title: Minimum Swaps to Make Unique and Palindromic Substring

statement.markdown:
Given a string s, return the minimum number of swaps to make all substrings unique and palindromic.

A string is palindromic if it reads the same backward as forward.

The string is unique if no two substrings are equal.

Example: input s = 'abaac', output should be 2. Explanation: 'abaac' can be broken down into substrings ['a', 'b', 'aa', 'c'], which must be rearranged into unique and palindromic substrings ['a', 'b', 'a', 'c']. 2 swaps are required.
statement.outputFormat: Output format: integer number of swaps

hints.text: First hint text: Use a hashmap to store substrings as keys and their counts as values.
Second hint text: Iterate through the hashmap and count the number of substrings that can be made unique and palindromic by swapping letters.
Third hint text: Use a sliding window approach to generate all possible substrings of the input string.

editorial.title: Minimum Swaps to Make Unique and Palindromic Substring
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a sliding window approach to generate all possible substrings of the input string and then count the number of unique and palindromic substrings.

testCases.input: First line count, second line elements (stdin match)
